title: Fermented Portuguese Lamb Casserole
description: A hearty, fermented, and crumbly casserole featuring lamb, fajita seasoning, and a dairy-free friendly twist on traditional Portuguese cuisine. This cozy dish is perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: non-dairy cheese shreds
ingredients.notes: such as soy cheese or vegan mozzarella

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the lamb and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the fajita seasoning, onions, and garlic to the pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the port wine and vegetable broth to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb mixture for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls apart easily.
While the lamb is braising, prepare the sauerkraut and potatoes. In a large bowl, combine the chopped sauerkraut and sliced potatoes.
After the lamb has finished braising, stir in the sauerkraut and potato mixture. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the lamb and sauerkraut mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish. Top with non-dairy cheese shreds.
Bake in the preheated oven for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
